description_long: This sculpture depicts two stylized human figures in a moment of interaction, both rendered in a blocky, geometric style. One figure is seated on a rectangular plinth with one knee raised, while the other figure sits on the ground with arms resting on their knees, facing the first. The forms are simplified, with minimal facial features and a rough, chiseled texture that emphasizes the stone material. The figures are positioned close together, suggesting a sense of conversation or contemplation. The base is a simple rectangular slab, supporting both figures and anchoring the composition. The overall appearance is monolithic and solid, with a muted gray coloration typical of carved stone.
description: A rough-textured stone sculpture of two abstract human figures, one seated on a plinth and the other on the ground, facing each other in a contemplative pose.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures in profile, with the seated figure on the left and the plinth-seated figure on the right, highlighting their interaction.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth of the plinth and the forward-leaning posture of the right figure, with the left figure's face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the broad back of the plinth-seated figure and the back of the ground-seated figure, with less detail on their faces.
description_view_3: Angled rear view focusing on the back and side of the ground-seated figure, with the plinth-seated figure partially visible.
attribute_subject_matter: two abstract human figures in conversation
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
